note: XIIGen own infrastructure capabilities. FT-100–FT-107 confirmed by FLOW-36 Phase A. FT-108–FT-109 from FLOW-35 (implemented). FT-110–FT-115 from FLOW-36 (planned). productScope=xiigen-capability: these features power XIIGen itself, not client flows. Signals use XIIGEN_VARIANT mode.

features.name: MultiTenantFlowOrchestration
features.description: Core tenant-scoped flow execution engine: resolves tenant context via AsyncLocalStorage, dispatches flow phases in order, enforces phase gate contracts, and emits lifecycle events per DNA-9.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal. Requires AsyncLocalStorage for tenant context propagation. Porting would expose core orchestration internals to an untrusted platform runtime. Architectural boundary violation.

features.name: GraphRAGTwoLayerSeeding
features.description: Two-layer GraphRAG population protocol: Layer 1 seeds node semantics (task types, services, patterns), Layer 2 seeds cross-flow dependency edges. Layer 2 is blocked until Layer 1 is COMPLETE (CF-743).
portingConstraints: Requires RAG fabric with vector indexing for semantic node search
Layer ordering is enforced via database sentinel — port must preserve CF-743 gate

features.name: ImplementationStatusTracking
features.description: Per-family implementation run tracker with SETNX idempotency locks. State machine: PENDING → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED | FAILED | NEEDS_REVIEW. Composite key: tenantId::flowId::familyId::runId (CF-739).
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal state machine. Requires access to XIIGen's internal bootstrap sentinel state. Porting would expose idempotency lock mechanism to an external platform runtime.

features.name: FamilyMetaImplementationLoop
features.description: AI-driven bounded generation loop per family: assembles fresh ContextPack each attempt, generates code via AI, runs FiveArbiterConsensusGate, injects feedback between rounds (CF-750), escalates on max retries.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal generation loop. IS XIIGen's code generation engine. Porting would expose generation internals to an external platform runtime. Architectural boundary violation.

features.name: FiveArbiterConsensusGate
features.description: Quality gate running 5 specialized arbiters in parallel (Promise.allSettled) covering architecture, security, DNA compliance, business logic, and integration. Quorum: ≥4/5 APPROVED required.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal consensus gate. Requires access to XIIGen's arbiter registry and internal evaluation state. Porting would expose AI generation quality control to an untrusted platform.

features.name: RegressionImpactAnalysis
features.description: Computes blast radius of a promoted flow via GraphRAG edge traversal. Implements 3-case promotion protocol: CASE_C (empty blast→ACTIVE), CASE_A (within threshold→PROMOTED→ACTIVE/REGRESSED), CASE_B (exceeds→EscalationBriefing).
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal analysis. Requires access to XIIGen's internal GraphRAG dependency graph and promotion state machine. Porting would expose regression analysis internals.

features.name: ContextPackAssembly
features.description: Assembles a TTL-governed ContextPack for a family implementation attempt: retrieves RAG context, reads flow contracts, checks existing pack staleness (CF-747). Stored before emit (DNA-8).
portingConstraints: TTL must come from FREEDOM config (flow33_context_pack_ttl_minutes)
RAG fabric required for similar-context retrieval

features.name: FeatureRegistryPortingDecisionGate
features.description: Cross-platform porting decision gate: evaluates feature signals, portingCandidate flag, blast radius, and tenant FREEDOM config to return APPROVE | DEFER | BLOCK | PROHIBITED for a given ftId × targetPlatform pair.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal porting governance. This IS the porting decision engine. Porting it to an external platform would expose XIIGen's architectural boundary enforcement logic.

features.name: RoundSummaryProcessor
features.description: Triggered by ArbitersComplete event. Collects all arbiter results for the current generation round and assembles a structured RoundSummary. Enriches with cost, token counts, model metadata. Persists before emit (DNA-8).
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal meta-arbitration. Requires access to XIIGen's internal arbiter registry and round state. Porting would expose generation quality collection internals.

features.name: MetaDecisionEngine
features.description: Triggered by RoundSummaryReady event. Applies all 5 meta-arbiter policies (SpendGovernor, SecurityCircuitBreaker, ImprovementDetector, ModelFitness, RoundController) in sequence. Produces final RoundDecision: CONTINUE | ESCALATE | HALT | ACCEPT.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal meta-arbiter. IS XIIGen's generation quality governance engine. Porting would expose spend governance, security circuit breaker, and model fitness evaluation to an external runtime. Architectural boundary violation.

features.name: FeatureExtractor
features.description: Extracts FT entries from source code or design input with portingCandidate classification and productScope assignment. Validates output against feature-manifest.schema.json v2.0.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal feature extraction. Requires access to XIIGen's internal feature registry and RAG indexes. Porting would expose FT extraction and classification logic to an external runtime.

features.name: FeatureSignalAggregator
features.description: Aggregates MODE_A (engine telemetry) and MODE_B (marketplace) signals per FT per platform. Evaluates porting threshold formulas.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal signal governance. Requires access to XIIGen's internal telemetry and porting threshold evaluation. Porting would expose signal aggregation and threshold logic.

features.name: PortingCostEstimator
features.description: Estimates porting effort for a specific FT to a target platform via RAG retrieval of platform constraint docs and historical porting data. Only runs when portingCandidate=true.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal cost estimation. Requires access to XIIGen's internal RAG indexes for platform constraints. Porting would expose internal cost model to an external runtime.

features.name: PlatformAdapterGenerator
features.description: Generates thin adapter code for target platform after PortingDecisionGate APPROVE. Adapter contains only platform-specific API calls — zero business logic.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal code generation. IS XIIGen's adapter code generator. Porting it to Figma/Canva would create a paradox — you cannot use an adapter generator running in Figma to generate Figma adapters.

features.name: PlatformSimulator
features.description: Simulates target platform runtime for generated adapter validation. Supports Figma and Canva mock runtimes. Local-only, zero cloud credentials.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal simulator infrastructure. Requires XIIGen's local mock runtime infrastructure. Porting the simulator to the platform it simulates creates a circular dependency.

features.name: FeaturePortingOrchestrator
features.description: Orchestrates the full porting pipeline for one FT: portingCandidate guard → cost estimation → decision gate → adapter generation → simulator. Emits per-step progress events.
features.portingCandidateReason: Engine-internal orchestration. IS XIIGen's porting pipeline coordinator. Requires access to all porting sub-services. Porting would expose the entire porting orchestration engine to an external platform.
